meta.title: BMW M10 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
meta.description: “The BMW M10 four-cylinder is tough, simple, and long-lived – but age takes its toll. Get honest UK specialist quotes and classic car advice today.”

twitter.title: BMW M10 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
twitter.description: The BMW M10 four-cylinder is tough, simple, and long-lived – but age takes its toll. Get honest UK specialist quotes and classic car advice today.

@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the M10

mainEntity.name: How much does an M10 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M10 typically costs £2,500–£4,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement and specification. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on labour rates and classic car access.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M10 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: We rate the M10 at 4.0 stars out of 5, based on an 88% confidence level from classic car specialists and owner surveys [ESTIMATED]. It is legendary for durability – but 40+ years of service mean age-related wear is the primary issue.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M10 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear (especially on high-mileage units) and valve guide wear are the most common failures. The single-row timing chain can stretch; the later twin-row chain is more robust.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the M10?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M10 powered the 1602, 2002, E21 316/318/320, E30 316/318i, and the E12/E28 518 series. It was BMW's workhorse four-cylinder for over 25 years.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M10 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance (regular oil changes, valve adjustments, and timing chain attention), the M10 can easily exceed 200,000 miles. Many original engines remain on the road today – they are exceptionally durable.

hero.h1: BMW M10 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW M10 is the four-cylinder that built BMW's reputation – tough, simple, and long-lived, though age-related wear is now a given. Backed by UK classic car data [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: Also fitted to Jensen-Healey, Lotus, Marcos, Opel (sold to other manufacturers)

verdictRating.confidence: 88% – based on classic car specialist reports and UK owner surveys.

rows.score: 16 (Simple, overbuilt design; most repairs are straightforward for any classic specialist)
16 (Excellent aftermarket support; many parts still available new or from specialist suppliers)
12 (Recon cost ~45–60% of lowest typical value (e.g. scruffy 316) → 12)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M10 is one of BMW's toughest engines – but age and neglect are now the main threats – buy with documented history and compression figures.
verdictRating.bestFor: Classic BMW enthusiasts who appreciate simplicity, durability, and easy DIY maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You want modern power, fuel economy, or don't want to maintain a 40+ year old classic.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M10 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: The M10 was sold to other manufacturers – ensure you're buying the correct version for your application (mounts, flywheel pattern, sump configuration vary).
